trend_objects.label: Soft Tailoring Revival
trend_objects.summary: Structured yet fluid blazers and suits with relaxed silhouettes, often paired with silk blouses. Emphasis on quality fabric drape and refined construction. Strong presence across Dior and Celine lookbooks shared by luxury fashion KOLs.
trend_objects.ai_reasoning: Multiple high-engagement posts from fashion KOLs in Beijing and Shanghai showcase soft tailoring as the dominant styling language for spring 2026. Consistent engagement pattern across 9 posts indicates sustained interest rather than a single viral moment.

trend_objects.ai_reasoning: Floral print dressing is experiencing a clear spring surge with consistent posting cadence from March 10 onwards. The imagery references Dior's Granville garden heritage and Miss Dior aesthetic, making this trend highly brand-congruent.

trend_objects.ai_reasoning: A growing cluster of content explicitly celebrating Dior's atelier craftsmanship in RTW pieces. Snippet language focuses on construction details rather than styling — indicating a more sophisticated, heritage-conscious audience.

trend_objects.label: Vintage Denim Revival
trend_objects.summary: 90s-inspired denim styling — wide-leg jeans, denim-on-denim, distressed finishes. Primarily young casual creators, occasionally paired with luxury accessories.
trend_objects.ai_reasoning: Strong general trend signal but last post dates indicate this peaked in late January. No recent content in the past 3 weeks from Dior-relevant creators.

trend_objects.ai_reasoning: Color blocking peaked in late January/early February. Momentum appears to have subsided. Not aligned with Dior's more restrained color palette approach.

trend_objects.summary: Neutral, unbranded minimalism — cream, oat, stone tones in simple silhouettes. Subdued styling with focus on fabric quality. Last active content from mid-February — appears to have plateaued.
trend_objects.ai_reasoning: Minimalist content from February has not carried momentum into March. Post dates are all pre-March, suggesting this trend has peaked. Engagement remains medium.

trend_objects.label: Straw and Raffia Summer Bags
trend_objects.summary: Natural material bags — straw, raffia, and wicker — positioned as luxury summer accessories. Content volume is low and trend feels premature for spring. Only 4 posts from early March.
trend_objects.ai_reasoning: While natural material bags are a real summer luxury trend, the signal is too early and too thin for March briefing. Only 4 posts with borderline engagement. Category timing does not align with current retail context.
